passages.title: Orientation of birds
passages.source_html:
Orientation of birds


A 


For many of us, the way birds are able to orientate is both astounding and difficult to appreciate fully. For instance, the annual migration of the golden plover of the Pacific takes it from Alaska to Hawaii on a flight of well over 3000 kilometres, and if it were to deviate by only one degree, it would miss the island on which it nests.


B 


The first systematic studies on orientation in birds were made possible by the 'homing instinct' exhibited by so many species. Birds are caught at a time when they show an attachment to their territory, especially during the nesting season. They are taken to some spot, released, and the percentage of returns is recorded. The distance can be varied, and the direction, as well as the method of transporting them, and then the influence of climatic and other factors on their ability to find their way home can be studied. These experiments have shown a wide variation in ability to home, and three types of homing behaviour have been identified.


C 


In the first type, birds methodically explore the area in which they are released until they pick up some familiar feature, and then they quickly find their way back to the nest. Such birds possess a highly developed visual memory, as experiments with pigeons have shown. Domestic pigeons have been trained to peck at a certain point on an aerial photograph, with a system of rewards, and four years later the birds were still able to respond to this training when placed on the aerial photograph. Birds' eyes have a power of resolution two to three times greater than ours, enabling them to pick up very fine details. If a bird uses only this type of homing behaviour, however, it can only succeed if the point of release is not too far away. If the birds are transported 800 kilometres from their nest, it is only by good fortune that they find their way back as a result of long exploratory flights. Usually, the area known to a bird is its feeding territory. Released within this area, the birds soon make their return; release them outside it and far fewer return. However, if a bird is released for a second time in the same place, its visual memory comes into play, and the bird, no longer requiring tedious exploratory flights, will return much more quickly.


D 


The second type of homing behaviour is shown by birds that are capable of choosing their flight direction and holding to it for the rest of their journey. How do they decide what direction to take They appear to choose their normal migration direction even if they are released in a different place from their usual starting point. If, for example, birds which normally fly to the north-east to reach latitude 45 degrees north are released at that latitude, they will immediately start flying north-east anyway. So if they're released further to the west, they maintain the correct direction, but fly west of their destination, and so fail to arrive.


E 


The third type of homing behaviour shows the highest degree of orientation. Released at one point, the birds immediately take stock of it, compare its position with that of the nest, decide on the direction and fly off. This happens even if the birds are in a country right off their migration routes, where they have never been before. In one example, a laysan albatross returned to its nesting area on Midway Island in the middle of the Pacific having flown over 5000 kilometres from the west coast of the USA in just over ten days. This is a perfect example of the third type of homing, for the albatross clearly couldn't rely on any landmarks over the vast expanse of the Pacific Ocean.


F 


The percentage of successful birds varies greatly, being highest in those species with a strong migratory behaviour. Thus the lesser black-backed gull is more migratory than the herring gull and more often reaches home'. Great migrants such as the swift have the highest percentage of returns. In one case, seven out of nine alpine swifts were recaptured at their nests. After being displaced some 1,400 kilometres, one made the journey in three days.


G 


What part does heredity play in all this? Two research studies suggest that instinctive, i.e. genetically inherited, behaviour patterns play a part in navigation. The first was carried out by Ernstzx Schüz and it is highly significant. Schüz caught first-year European storks and released them later, after the departure of the adult storks at a time when they normally make their south-west autumn migration to Africa. The recaptures showed that, in spite of the fact that there were no adults to guide them, the birds unanimously headed south-west. This was an almost striking finding, for it showed that the birds had an innate and unlearned attraction for the African wintering area that they have occupied for thousands of years.


H 


The case of starlings is a little different. These birds have a great aptitude for homing, but this behaviour differs in the different age groups. Birds that were shifted to the south-east of their normal migration route split into two lots. The adults, in full possession of their gift for orientation, found their wintering area by modifying their direction by 90 degrees, whereas the juveniles sought their winter quarters to the south-east of their real position.

groups.title: Questions 14-26
groups.source_html:
Questions 14 - 18


Complete the summary. Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS in each gap.

14

Types of homing behaviour

First type:

Birds rely on their sophisticated  . However, they are generally most successful if they are released within their feeding territory.

正确答案： 14 visual memory
Question 14

审题:  鸟类依赖它们复杂的什么来找到巢穴？ 

 

定位关键词: sophisticated，rely，first type


对应原文:  C段，In the first type, birds methodically explore the area in which they are released until they pick up some familiar feature, and then they quickly find their way back to the nest. Such birds possess a highly developed visual memory, as experiments with pigeons have shown.


解题思路:  C段指出, 在第一种类型中，鸟类有条不紊地探索它们被释放的区域，直到它们掌握了一些熟悉的特征，然后它们迅速找到返回巢穴的路。这类鸟类拥有高度发达的视觉记忆。因此第一种类型的鸟类依赖他们的记忆来寻找巢穴。

15

Second type:

Birds select their accustomed  , no matter where they are released. As a result, they may miss their  .

正确答案： 15 migration direction  |  16 destination
Question 15

审题:  鸟类选择它们习惯的什么来进行飞行？  


定位关键词: accustomed，second type


对应原文: D段，The second type of homing behaviour is shown by birds that are capable of choosing their flight direction and holding to it for the rest of their journey. How do they decide what direction to take They appear to choose their normal migration direction even if they are released in a different place from their usual starting point.


解题思路:D段指出, 第二种归巢行为表现在鸟类能够选择飞行方向，并在剩下的旅程中一直保持这个方向。即使在不同的地方被释放，它们似乎也会选择一直以来已经习惯的迁徙方向。

Question 16

审题:  由于这一行为，它们可能会错过它们的什么？  


定位关键词: miss，second type


对应原文: D段，If, for example, birds which normally fly to the north-east to reach latitude 45 degrees north are released at that latitude, they will immediately start flying north-east anyway. So if they're release further to the west, they maintain the correct direction, but fly west of their destination, and so fail to arrive.


解题思路:D段指出, 通常往东北飞到北纬45度的鸟类，在那个纬度被释放，它们无论如何都会立即开始往东北飞。因此，如果它们被释放到更远的西部，它们保持正确的方向，但飞向目的地的西部，因此会错过目的地而无法到达。

17

Third type:

Birds orientate correctly, even when they are released in an unfamiliar place and have no  to make use of. One bird with this type of skill is the  .

正确答案： 17 landmarks  |  18 laysan albatross##albatross
Question 17

审题:  即使它们在一个陌生的地方被释放，并且没有什么可以利用，它们也能正确定向。  


定位关键词:an unfamiliar place，make use of，third type


对应原文:  E段，This is a perfect example of the third type of homing, for the albatross clearly couldn't rely on any landmarks over the vast expanse of the Pacific Ocean.


解题思路:  E段指出, 第三种归巢行为表现出最高程度的定向能力。这种鸟类在陌生的环境，无法依赖 landmarks（地标）进行定向，也能成功归巢。

Question 18

审题: 具有这种技能的鸟类之一是什么？  


定位关键词: with this type of skill


对应原文: E段，In one example, a laysan albatross returned to its nesting area on Midway Island in the middle of the Pacific having flown over 5000 kilometres from the west coast of the USA in just over ten days. 


解题思路:E段指出, 具有这种归巢技能的鸟类之一是albatross（信天翁）。


Questions 19 - 22

Look at the eight paragraphs (A - H). Which paragraph contains the following information? You may choose any letter more than once.

Question / ParagraphABCDEFG
19

19 the effects of distance on some birds' ability to find their nests
✅ 正确答案：C

审题: 哪个段落提到距离对一些鸟类找到巢穴的能力的影响。  


定位关键词: distance，ability，effects


对应原文: C段，If the birds are transported 800 kilometres from their nest, it is only by good fortune that they find their way back as a result of long exploratory flights.


解题思路:C段指出, 如果这些鸟被运送到离巢800公里远的地方，它们能找到回来的路只有靠运气。这段话说明了距离对鸟类归巢的影响。
20

20 a methodology for testing the general ability of birds to find their nests
✅ 正确答案：B

审题: 哪个段落提到来用于测试鸟类找到巢穴的一般能力的方法。 

 

定位关键词: methodology，testing，ability


对应原文:  B段，Birds are caught at a time when they show an attachment to their territory, especially during the nesting season. They are taken to some spot, released, and the percentage of returns is recorded.


解题思路:B段指出, 鸟类在表现出对领地的依恋时被捕获，尤其是在筑巢季节。他们被带到某个地点，释放，并记录回报率。这段话描述了测试鸟类归巢能力的方法。


同义替换：find their nests = attachment to their territory
21

21 one aspect of physical ability in humans and birds
✅ 正确答案：C

审题: 哪个段落讲述了人类和鸟类在某些身体能力上的对比。  


定位关键词: physical ability，humans and birds


对应原文: C段，Birds' eyes have a power of resolution two to three times greater than ours, enabling them to pick up very fine details. If a bird uses only this type of homing behaviour, however, it can only succeed if the point of release is not too far away. 


解题思路:C段指出, 鸟类眼睛的分辨能力是人类的两到三倍。这段话描述了人类和鸟类的视力对比。
22

22 how some birds' migration was delayed for experimental purposes
✅ 正确答案：G

审题: 哪个段落提到了鸟类迁徙因实验而被推迟的情况。  


定位关键词: migration，delayed，experimental purposes


对应原文: G段，Schüz caught first-year European storks and released them later, after the departure of the adult storks at a time when they normally make their south-west autumn migration to Africa. 


解题思路:  G段指出, Schüz捕获了一岁的欧洲鹳，并在成年鹳离开后将它们放生。这段话描述了实验中延迟了白鹳的迁徙。


同义替换：delayed=  later

Questions 23 - 26


Choose the correct point which the author wishes to illustrate, (A - G), for each type of bird. 


List of points which author wishes to illustrate

A an ability to orientate without previous training

B the speed at which birds fly

C the ability to remember things seen previously

D the effect of age on homing ability

E the strength required to fly a great distance

F a high success rate in finding nests

G the importance of seasonal cues for migrating birds


Question / ParagraphABCDEFG
23

23 domestic pigeon
✅ 正确答案：C

定位关键词: domestic pigeon


对应原文: C段，Domestic pigeons have been trained to peck at a certain point on an aerial photograph, with a system of rewards, and four years later the birds were still able to respond to this training when placed on the aerial photograph. 


解题思路:C段指出, 驯养的鸽子被训练去啄航拍照片上的某一点，并有奖励制度。说明家鸽展示了记住以前见过的事物的能力。
24

24 alpine swift
✅ 正确答案：F

审题:  高山雨燕展示了哪种能力？  


定位关键词: alpine swift


对应原文: F段，Great migrants such as the swift have the highest percentage of returns. In one case, seven out of nine alpine swifts were recaptured at their nests.


解题思路:  F段指出, 高山雨燕（swift）的返巢率最高。在一个案例中，九只高山雨燕中有七只在它们的巢穴中被重新捕获。
25

25 European stork
✅ 正确答案：A

审题:  欧洲白鹳展示了哪种能力？  


定位关键词: European stork


对应原文: G段，Schuz caught first-year European storks and released them later, after the departure of the adult storks at a time when they normally make their south-west autumn migration to Africa. The recaptures showed that, in spite of the fact that there were no adults to guide them, the birds unanimously headed south-west.


解题思路:G段指出, 重新捕获的结果表明，尽管没有成年鸟来引导它们，但这些鸟一致向西南方向飞去。说明白鹳展示了天生的方向感。
26

26 starling
✅ 正确答案：D

审题: 椋鸟展示了哪种能力？  


定位关键词: starling


对应原文: H段，Birds that were shifted to the south-east of their normal migration route split into two lots. The adults, in full possession of their gift for orientation, found their wintering area by modifying their direction by 90 degrees, whereas the juveniles sought their winter quarters to the south-east of their real position.


解题思路:  H段指出, 被转移到正常迁徙路线东南方向的鸟类被分成两组。成年企鹅完全拥有方向感，通过将方向调整90度来找到它们的越冬区，而幼企鹅则在它们实际位置的东南方向寻找它们的越冬区。椋鸟展示了年龄对归巢能力的影响。
